document.title: Academic Subject Learning Profile
document.subtitle: Bachelor of Architecture and Master of Planning
document.summary_policy: Summarizes what was taught in each subject; does not reproduce the syllabi verbatim.
document.optional_subject_policy: Elective and optional subjects are included only where confirmed by the candidate.

elective_choices.chosen_subject_or_scope: Urban Management: Integrated Urban Eco-System Management
Parameters: Regional Planning; Processes and Principles

courses.coverage_summary: Orientation to the architectural profession; principles and elements of design; functionality of space; 2D pattern-making and 3D form exploration; anthropometric understanding of personal spaces; measured drawing of an existing or historic building; short design exercises such as kiosks, shelters, stalls and similar small-scale functional spaces.
learning_coverage: Orientation to the architectural profession
principles and elements of design
functionality of space
2D pattern-making and 3D form exploration
anthropometric understanding of personal spaces
measured drawing of an existing or historic building
short design exercises such as kiosks, shelters, stalls and similar small-scale functional spaces

courses.coverage_summary: Elementary building construction through tools, carpentry joints, brick terminology and bonds, quoins, piers, corbelling and decorative brickwork; stone masonry; foundations, damp proof courses and brick or stone arches; basic material studies of bricks and timber.
learning_coverage: Elementary building construction through tools, carpentry joints, brick terminology and bonds, quoins, piers, corbelling and decorative brickwork
stone masonry
foundations, damp proof courses and brick or stone arches
basic material studies of bricks and timber

courses.coverage_summary: Basic building materials and building science; climate in architecture; climatic elements, zones, macroclimate and microclimate; thermal behaviour of buildings and materials; thermal comfort; solar geometry, shading, daylight, wind, ventilation, passive systems, site selection and material properties in relation to climate.
learning_coverage: Basic building materials and building science
climate in architecture
climatic elements, zones, macroclimate and microclimate
thermal behaviour of buildings and materials
thermal comfort
solar geometry, shading, daylight, wind, ventilation, passive systems, site selection and material properties in relation to climate

courses.coverage_summary: Drafting instruments, sheet layout, lettering, line types and scales; orthographic projections of points, lines, planes and solids; development of surfaces; sections of solids; and introductory sciagraphy for shadow construction.
learning_coverage: Drafting instruments, sheet layout, lettering, line types and scales
orthographic projections of points, lines, planes and solids
development of surfaces
sections of solids
and introductory sciagraphy for shadow construction

courses.coverage_summary: Basic workshop skills for representing architectural ideas: wood joinery, pipe and sleeve joints, welded and nut-bolt metal joints, model-making tools, block and detailed models, construction models, interior models and experimentation with materials at different scales.
learning_coverage: Basic workshop skills for representing architectural ideas: wood joinery, pipe and sleeve joints, welded and nut-bolt metal joints, model-making tools, block and detailed models, construction models, interior models and experimentation with materials at different scales.

courses.coverage_summary: Fundamentals of structural behaviour including stress-strain relationships, Hooke's law, elastic properties, centre of gravity, moment of inertia, two-dimensional force systems, static equilibrium, friction, shear force and bending moment in basic beams.
learning_coverage: Fundamentals of structural behaviour including stress-strain relationships, Hooke's law, elastic properties, centre of gravity, moment of inertia, two-dimensional force systems, static equilibrium, friction, shear force and bending moment in basic beams.

courses.coverage_summary: Surveying as a tool for reading land and topography; chain surveying, compass surveying, levelling, plane tabling, contouring, theodolite surveying, tachometric surveying, setting out of building works and interpretation/preparation of contour maps.
learning_coverage: Surveying as a tool for reading land and topography
chain surveying, compass surveying, levelling, plane tabling, contouring, theodolite surveying, tachometric surveying, setting out of building works and interpretation/preparation of contour maps

courses.coverage_summary: Devices for visual communication, cameras and photographic equipment; communication graphics, typography, composition, translation of concepts into word-image-layout formats, and the use of photography as a design presentation and analytical tool.
learning_coverage: Devices for visual communication, cameras and photographic equipment
communication graphics, typography, composition, translation of concepts into word-image-layout formats, and the use of photography as a design presentation and analytical tool

courses.coverage_summary: Introductory digital tools for documentation, presentation and drawing; use of scanners, printers and plotters; AutoCAD as a drafting tool; and office/presentation applications such as word processing, spreadsheets, slides and page layout.
learning_coverage: Introductory digital tools for documentation, presentation and drawing
use of scanners, printers and plotters
AutoCAD as a drafting tool
and office/presentation applications such as word processing, spreadsheets, slides and page layout

courses.coverage_summary: Introductory academic writing through research documentation, report writing formats, APA and Chicago style references, bibliographies, footnotes and citations, applied to the study of Indian arts.
learning_coverage: Introductory academic writing through research documentation, report writing formats, APA and Chicago style references, bibliographies, footnotes and citations, applied to the study of Indian arts.

courses.coverage_summary: Art in relation to architecture; composition and design basics; colour theory; aesthetic appreciation; exercises in shapes, natural patterns, collage, rapid sketching, nature studies and architectural graphic expression.
learning_coverage: Art in relation to architecture
composition and design basics
colour theory
aesthetic appreciation
exercises in shapes, natural patterns, collage, rapid sketching, nature studies and architectural graphic expression

courses.coverage_summary: Design projects responding to different climatic conditions, land and landforms, built/open space relations and vernacular/local material contexts; complex repetitive-space projects such as schools, hostels, shops or offices; circulation, parking, services and structural feasibility through site analysis and block models.
learning_coverage: Design projects responding to different climatic conditions, land and landforms, built/open space relations and vernacular/local material contexts
complex repetitive-space projects such as schools, hostels, shops or offices
circulation, parking, services and structural feasibility through site analysis and block models

courses.coverage_summary: Construction details for doors, windows, timber and bamboo roofs, trusses, staircases, lift wells and partitions; materials including glass, plastics, paints, varnishes, floor finishes and roofing materials; integration of construction details with design studio work.
learning_coverage: Construction details for doors, windows, timber and bamboo roofs, trusses, staircases, lift wells and partitions
materials including glass, plastics, paints, varnishes, floor finishes and roofing materials
integration of construction details with design studio work

courses.coverage_summary: Ancient world and Indian architecture: Stone Age to Neolithic settlements, Egyptian, Mesopotamian, Greek, Roman, Early Christian, Byzantine, Romanesque, Gothic and Central Asian architecture; Indus Valley planning, Vedic culture, Buddhist architecture, North and South Indian temple forms and Jain architecture.
learning_coverage: Ancient world and Indian architecture: Stone Age to Neolithic settlements, Egyptian, Mesopotamian, Greek, Roman, Early Christian, Byzantine, Romanesque, Gothic and Central Asian architecture
Indus Valley planning, Vedic culture, Buddhist architecture, North and South Indian temple forms and Jain architecture

courses.coverage_summary: Metric drawings and projections including isometric, axonometric and oblique views; perspective drawing, one-, two- and three-point perspectives, perspectives of furniture and residences; innovative perspective methods; interior perspectives; texture, furniture, vehicles and human figure representation; rendered presentation drawings.
learning_coverage: Metric drawings and projections including isometric, axonometric and oblique views
perspective drawing, one-, two- and three-point perspectives, perspectives of furniture and residences
innovative perspective methods
interior perspectives
texture, furniture, vehicles and human figure representation
rendered presentation drawings

courses.coverage_summary: Advanced model-making sequence from base preparation to layout cutting, joining, fixing and finishing; models for stairs, curtain glazing, pergolas, space frames and landscapes; detailed building models with colour, texture, landscape and human figures.
learning_coverage: Advanced model-making sequence from base preparation to layout cutting, joining, fixing and finishing
models for stairs, curtain glazing, pergolas, space frames and landscapes
detailed building models with colour, texture, landscape and human figures

courses.coverage_summary: Deflection of beams, determinate and indeterminate structures, load transfer, three-moment theorem, fixed and continuous beams, slope-deflection and moment distribution methods; design philosophies; analysis and design of reinforced concrete beam sections, shear, bond, beam failure mechanisms and concrete construction basics.
learning_coverage: Deflection of beams, determinate and indeterminate structures, load transfer, three-moment theorem, fixed and continuous beams, slope-deflection and moment distribution methods
design philosophies
analysis and design of reinforced concrete beam sections, shear, bond, beam failure mechanisms and concrete construction basics

courses.coverage_summary: Elementary building services for water supply, sanitation, drainage and roads; water sources, storage, consumption and fixtures; sanitary fittings, drainage systems, septic tanks, manholes, house drains and sewer gradients; road types and drainage elements.
learning_coverage: Elementary building services for water supply, sanitation, drainage and roads
water sources, storage, consumption and fixtures
sanitary fittings, drainage systems, septic tanks, manholes, house drains and sewer gradients
road types and drainage elements

courses.coverage_summary: Photography, digital imaging and computer visualisation for architectural presentation; image design, model photography, digital manipulation, rendering, lighting simulation, animation, scanning, raster graphics, virtual reality and tools such as InDesign, Illustrator and SketchUp.
learning_coverage: Photography, digital imaging and computer visualisation for architectural presentation
image design, model photography, digital manipulation, rendering, lighting simulation, animation, scanning, raster graphics, virtual reality and tools such as InDesign, Illustrator and SketchUp

courses.coverage_summary: Architectural software applications with emphasis on AutoCAD; 2D drafting commands, layers, text/dimension styles, polylines, splines, external references, attributes, model space and paper space; working plan, elevation and section drafting; introduction to 3D modelling.
learning_coverage: Architectural software applications with emphasis on AutoCAD
2D drafting commands, layers, text/dimension styles, polylines, splines, external references, attributes, model space and paper space
working plan, elevation and section drafting
introduction to 3D modelling

courses.coverage_summary: Research into historic and contemporary Indian architecture and its social and cultural implications; topic framing, synopsis approval, site visits, interviews, library work and a 40-50 page research report on an area, period or building of significance.
learning_coverage: Research into historic and contemporary Indian architecture and its social and cultural implications
topic framing, synopsis approval, site visits, interviews, library work and a 40-50 page research report on an area, period or building of significance

courses.coverage_summary: Advanced architectural graphic sense through motifs, patterns, murals, book covers, 3D paper sculpture, tonal colour treatment, still life, perspective, rapid sketching, calligraphy, typography, media studies and rendering of building interiors and exteriors.
learning_coverage: Advanced architectural graphic sense through motifs, patterns, murals, book covers, 3D paper sculpture, tonal colour treatment, still life, perspective, rapid sketching, calligraphy, typography, media studies and rendering of building interiors and exteriors.

courses.coverage_summary: Design of structural spanning systems and urban-setting buildings; structural explorations such as arches, vaults, domes, trusses, space frames, folded slabs, shells, tensile and pneumatic systems; complex multi-storeyed design such as offices, malls, hotels, colleges or hospitals with circulation, construction and working drawing integration.
learning_coverage: Design of structural spanning systems and urban-setting buildings
structural explorations such as arches, vaults, domes, trusses, space frames, folded slabs, shells, tensile and pneumatic systems
complex multi-storeyed design such as offices, malls, hotels, colleges or hospitals with circulation, construction and working drawing integration

courses.coverage_summary: Modern construction using steel, aluminium, glass and composite materials; doors, windows, rolling shutters, fire/sound-proof doors, steel staircases and fire escapes; waterproofing systems; steel trusses, terracing, rainwater disposal and metal sections/materials used in buildings.
learning_coverage: Modern construction using steel, aluminium, glass and composite materials
doors, windows, rolling shutters, fire/sound-proof doors, steel staircases and fire escapes
waterproofing systems
steel trusses, terracing, rainwater disposal and metal sections/materials used in buildings

courses.coverage_summary: Medieval and modern world architecture with emphasis on India: Islamic, Renaissance, Baroque, Neo-classical and modern movements; Sultanate, provincial, Rajasthani, Mughal, colonial and New Delhi architecture studied with reference to climate, society, politics, construction, materials and style.
learning_coverage: Medieval and modern world architecture with emphasis on India: Islamic, Renaissance, Baroque, Neo-classical and modern movements
Sultanate, provincial, Rajasthani, Mughal, colonial and New Delhi architecture studied with reference to climate, society, politics, construction, materials and style

courses.coverage_summary: Human settlement patterns and their relevance to architecture; man-environment relations, primitive shelters, settlement origins, river valley settlements, Indus, Vedic, Nile, Chinese, Euphrates-Tigris civilisations, medieval towns, Renaissance planning and industrial revolution impacts.
learning_coverage: Human settlement patterns and their relevance to architecture

man-environment relations, primitive shelters, settlement origins, river valley settlements, Indus, Vedic, Nile, Chinese, Euphrates-Tigris civilisations, medieval towns, Renaissance planning and industrial revolution impacts

courses.coverage_summary: Quantities and estimates for medium-complexity building works; specifications, area classifications, preliminary and detailed estimates, bills of quantities, measurement methods, market rates, labour/material norms, CPWD/PWD schedules, rate analysis, tender notices and tender comparison.

courses.coverage_summary: Design of reinforced concrete slabs, columns, beams, portal frames, staircases, strip and isolated footings and retaining walls; soil mechanics, bearing capacity, sub-soil investigation, shallow and deep foundations; introductory steel connections, tension/compression members, girders and grillage foundations.

courses.coverage_summary: Integrated building and urban-scale design; group housing or large-scale multi-storey projects addressing movement, traffic, services, waste management, materials, lighting and landscape; urban design intervention with demography, land values, land use, open-built relations, surveys and study models.

courses.coverage_summary: Specialized construction details such as soundproof doors, bay windows, skylights, pile foundations and pile caps, glazed/lightweight/soundproof partitions, soft panelling, precast and prestressed/post-tensioned concrete members, modular construction and contemporary materials such as Corian, veneers, ETFE and light-gauge steel.

courses.coverage_summary: Intensive elective study in a language option offered through the Department of Foreign Languages. The selected elective was Language, with learning structured through lectures/tutorials/practical work, self-study, a report/presentation and viva voce.
learning_coverage: Intensive elective study in a language option offered through the Department of Foreign Languages. The selected elective was Language, with learning structured through lectures/tutorials/practical work, self-study, a report/presentation and viva voce.

courses.course_title: Elective-II: Energy Efficient Design of Building
courses.coverage_summary: Selected thesis-linked elective focusing on energy-efficient building design. The syllabus allowed Energy Efficiency as an elective area, developed through lectures/tutorials/self-study, detailed study of the selected component, report/presentation and viva; the profile focus includes passive design, energy-conscious services, materials, lighting, ventilation, rating systems and environmental performance.
learning_coverage: Selected thesis-linked elective focusing on energy-efficient building design. The syllabus allowed Energy Efficiency as an elective area, developed through lectures/tutorials/self-study, detailed study of the selected component, report/presentation and viva

competency_statement.architecture: Across the B.Arch curriculum, the candidate acquired architectural design capability from foundational composition and anthropometrics to housing, multi-storeyed buildings, urban design and thesis-level design resolution. The candidate also studied building construction, materials, structures, services, environmental performance, architectural history, graphics, digital tools, estimation, byelaws, management, professional practice and office training.
competency_statement.planning: Across the M.Plan curriculum, the candidate developed planning competencies in history and theory, data and survey techniques, transport, housing, environment, metropolitan and regional planning, infrastructure, geoinformatics, urban governance, disaster management, planning law, development finance, research methods, urban and regional studios and thesis research. The selected electives add focused coverage of jurisprudence for planning, integrated urban ecosystem management and regional planning processes/principles.

data_quality_notes: General Proficiency entries are not treated as academic subjects.
M.Plan audit courses are included and marked with is_audit_course=true.
Elective rows are marked with is_elective_or_optional=true and include the candidate-selected option.
learning_coverage is generated by splitting the profile coverage summary into topic-level items for downstream parsing.
